sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! SPACE produced a 26-frame animation with 1090 cells changing. The last frame changed 1088 cells. This is a much bigger animation than before. Let me look at the animation details more carefully.
The animation shows:
- Frame 0: 82 cells changed
- Frames 1-14: 16-32 cells changed per frame
- Frames 15-16: 64 cells changed
- Frames 17-25: 192-256 cells changed
- Frame 25: 1088 cells changed (final frame)
This is a significant animation. The object is described as "top-left (8,0) 56x64, 1088 cells". This covers rows 8-63, cols 0-63, which is almost the entire board except the top 8 rows.
Let me check the current board state to see what changed permanently.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Check current board state after SPACE
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print(f"Number of nodes: {len(seg)}")
for n in seg:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, {n['pixels']} cells")

# Check if the board changed permanently
if last_transition:
    before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
    after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']
    print(f"\nBefore: {len(before)} nodes, After: {len(after)} nodes")
    before_colors = {n['color'] for n in before}
    after_colors = {n['color'] for n in after}
    print(f"Before colors: {before_colors}")
    print(f"After colors: {after_colors}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
